title: FIT — NAICS 488310 Port and Harbor Operations

sections.title: Anchoring context
sections.content:
| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Commodity FP | Deliver operational grid-connected electrical energy storage assets as turnkey capital projects with single-source EPC + grid-code certification |
| Technology class | Large-Scale BESS EPC |
| Mechanism | Containerised Li-ion + PCS + MV + EMS under single EPC contract, VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 |
| Top underserved needs (imp/sat/opp) | N1 sizing 10/3/17 · N4 DSO blocker 10/3/17 · N7 RtB<15mo 10/3/17 · N10 VOB/VgV bid 9/2/16 · N25 AFIR miss 9/3/15 · N9 VdS fire 9/3/15 · N16 PCS ride-through 10/5/15 |

---

sections.title: Step 09 — Technology-Level Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
Six technology-scope features × single market (488310). Scores 1–10 per ODI dimension; Kano vs. commodity FP baseline + port-specific needs.

### 09a — Per-feature Kano + 9D ODI fit

| # | Feature | Kano | Time | Cost | Safety | Rel. | Skill | Stress | Pain | Conf. | Delight | **Overall** |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Containerized Li-ion BESS Integration | must_be | 7 | 7 | 4 | 8 | 4 | 5 | 5 | 7 | 3 | **5.56** |
| F2 | Bidirectional Power Conversion System | must_be | 6 | 8 | 5 | 9 | 3 | 4 | 4 | 7 | 3 | **5.44** |
| F3 | Medium-Voltage Grid Coupling | must_be | 5 | 6 | 7 | 9 | 4 | 6 | 3 | 7 | 2 | **5.44** |
| F4 | Energy Management System (EMS) | one_dim. | 7 | 9 | 4 | 7 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 7 | 5 | **6.33** |
| F5 | Grid-Code Certification Delivery | must_be | 9 | 6 | 8 | 7 | 6 | 9 | 7 | 9 | 3 | **7.11** |
| F6 | Civil Works & Site Preparation | must_be | 6 | 5 | 6 | 5 | 4 | 5 | 6 | 5 | 2 | **4.89** |

### 09a — Rationale (one-line per feature)

| # | Rationale (Kano · drivers · market evidence) |
|---|---|
| F1 | Container BESS is the commodity baseline (must_be). Primary drivers: Reliability (8, drives AFIR OPS uptime N14/N16), Time (7, modular deploy beats grid reinforcement A2). Safety muted (4) — salt-air + passenger-berth fire clearance add risk (N6/N9). |
| F2 | 4-quadrant PCS is table-stakes. Drivers: Reliability (9, ride-through under grid fault — N16), Cost (8, arbitrage + demand-shaving — N15). Skill/Pain low — operators don't touch PCS daily. |
| F3 | MV coupling is expected in any port EPC. Drivers: Reliability (9), Safety (7, protection relays prevent fault propagation into terminal). Time/Cost middling because MV cabinet is not the bottleneck — DSO capacity is (N4). |
| F4 | EMS fit varies by sophistication — one_dimensional. Drivers: Cost (9, multi-revenue stacking N2/N15), Stress (7, reduces manual dispatch anxiety), Delight (5, the "this just works" lever for N18 dispatch-priority drift). |
| F5 | Highest-scoring feature — Cert delivery directly answers the #1 binding blocker (N7, N9, N11, N12). Drivers: Time (9, critical-path compression), Confidence (9, tender-evaluation gate), Stress (9, N25 AFIR-miss anxiety). Must_be per commodity FP. |
| F6 | Generic civil scope; any EPC provides it. Drivers: Reliability (5 — foundation + fencing are hygiene). Low Delight (2). Must_be but weakest overall differentiator. |

### 09b — Cross-feature validation

| Check | Status | Note |
|---|---|---|
| Overall_fit arithmetic | ✅ | Recomputed 5.56 / 5.44 / 5.44 / 6.33 / 7.11 / 4.89 |
| Safety ≥ 7 ⇒ Confidence ≥ 3 | ✅ | F5: Safety 8, Confidence 9 |
| Skill ≥ 7 ⇒ Stress ≥ 3 | n/a | No feature scored Skill ≥ 7 |
| No feature scored 10 all dimensions | ✅ | — |
| Feature names spec-visible, 2–6 words | ✅ | — |
| Kano consistent with commodity FP | ✅ | Commodity-fulfilling features = must_be; EMS optimization excess = one_dim |

---

sections.title: Step 10 — Product-Related Needs (Burleson Product Jobs)
sections.content:
Needs that exist BECAUSE Mein Solar uses containerised Li-ion + MV coupling (would disappear with flow-battery, flywheel, HV direct-connect, or diesel).

### 10a — Product-related needs

| # | Statement | Source constraint | Aff. markets | Imp | Sat |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PR1 | Minimize the likelihood of IP55/66 enclosure breach from salt-air chloride corrosion (IEC 60721-3 C5-M/CX) accelerating BMS/PCS electronics failure | Ambient Temperature Envelope + Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | 488310 | 8.5 | 3.5 |
| PR2 | Minimize the likelihood of Feuerwehr/VdS fire-clearance refusal for Li-ion containers sited within cruise/passenger-berth exclusion zones | Fire-Protection Clearance | 488310 | 9.0 | 3.0 |
| PR3 | Minimize the time to redesign container layout when ISPS-restricted-zone or port-police clearances reduce the available quayside footprint below 40 m²/MWh | Land Footprint and Siting | 488310 | 8.5 | 3.5 |
| PR4 | Minimize the likelihood of accelerated cycle-life degradation from sustained high C-rate (ferry fast-charge, STS regen) exceeding LFP 0.5 C design point | Cycle Lifetime + Round-Trip Energy Losses | 488310 | 8.0 | 4.0 |
| PR5 | Minimize the likelihood of MV protection-relay mis-coordination between DSO feeder, terminal ring-bus and OPS ship-side breakers causing nuisance trips during fault ride-through | MV Grid Connection Capacity | 488310 | 8.5 | 4.0 |
| PR6 | Minimize the likelihood of firmware-version drift between BMS, PCS, EMS and port SCADA/TOS that blocks re-commissioning after a single subsystem update | Grid Code Certification Lead Time | 488310 | 8.0 | 3.0 |
| PR7 | Minimize the time to recover contracted SoC window after a high-C-rate cruise-berth discharge event before the next vessel call | Round-Trip Energy Losses + Cycle Lifetime | 488310 | 7.5 | 4.0 |
| PR8 | Minimize the likelihood of EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 Battery Passport / EoL reporting non-compliance under public-asset ownership | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | 488310 | 8.0 | 3.5 |
| PR9 | Minimize the likelihood of HVAC condensate / salt-fog ingress degrading liquid-cooling loop seals and triggering thermal derating in summer berth peaks | Ambient Temperature Envelope | 488310 | 7.5 | 4.0 |

### 10b — Validation

| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| Litmus: each need disappears if tech ≠ containerised Li-ion on coastal MV grid | ✅ (flow-batt removes PR4/PR7; HV direct-connect removes PR5/PR6; non-Li chemistry removes PR2/PR8) |
| Source constraint ∈ Step 07 list | ✅ |
| Affected-markets plausibility (salt-air ⇒ port only) | ✅ |
| No duplicate with market needs (Step 06 / JTBD) | ✅ (distinct from N1/N4/N7 which are market-inherent) |
| Completeness: every hard constraint generates ≥1 need | ✅ (MV cap → PR5; siting → PR3; fire → PR2; thermal runaway → PR2; temp → PR1/PR9; cycle life → PR4/PR7; cert lead → PR6; supply chain → PR8) |
| No importance<3 AND satisfaction>7 entries | ✅ |

---

sections.title: Step 11 — Vendor Feature-Market Fit (Mein Solar)
sections.content:
Three vendor-scope features × market 488310. Best-incumbent baseline per need cluster taken from JTBD `incumbent_anchors`: Siemens SI, ABB P&T, Wärtsilä Energy, Cavotec, Schneider EcoStruxure, Fluence.

### 11a — Vendor vs. best incumbent

| # | Feature (generic name) | Kano | Vendor | Incumbent | Best incumbent | Advantage |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V1 | Integrated PV+Storage Design Authority | attractive | 7 | 4 | Schneider EcoStruxure (hybrid bundled) | **superior** |
| V2 | HPC-Charging Buffer Configuration | one_dim. | 6 | 6 | ABB Ports & Terminals (HPC portfolio) | parity |
| V3 | Single-Source EPC Contract Vehicle | attractive | 7 | 8 | Siemens Smart Infrastructure (full turnkey port) | **inferior** |

### 11a — Rationale

| # | Rationale |
|---|---|
| V1 | Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency allows one VDE-AR-N 4110 dossier, one MV cabinet and one EMS covering rooftop PV + quayside BESS — directly attacks N2 (bankable multi-revenue model) and N14 (OPS uptime when PV fills SoC between berths). Schneider/Siemens bundle PV+storage but without PV-EPC craftsmanship in the same business unit. Attractive because port authorities don't expect PV-heritage EPCs to also deliver BESS at scale. Spec gap: Mein Solar 4.5 MW / 9 MWh reference + PV EPC backlog vs. Schneider bundled reference at port scale is sparse in DACH. |
| V2 | Mein Solar's HPC-buffer feature (350 kW × 6 on 400 kVA feeder) matches ABB's commercial HPC-BESS integration for reach-stacker / ferry-charging (N4, N15). ABB ships shipped 20 MWh at ECT Rotterdam; Mein Solar lacks port reference. Parity — neither clearly wins until a reference project closes the gap. |
| V3 | Siemens SI can wrap OPS (IEC 80005-1), MV feeder (VDE-AR-N 4110), BESS and TOS uplink under one Smart Infrastructure master agreement — a capability Mein Solar does not yet possess at port-public-tender scale. N10 (VOB/VgV bid without public-port reference) and N25 (AFIR-miss anxiety) both favour Siemens unless Mein Solar joint-bids. Inferior until a first HHLA/NPorts/Duisport anchor closes. |

### 11b — Objectivity review

| Check | Status | Note |
|---|---|---|
| Distribution: superior/parity/inferior | ✅ 1/1/1 | Balanced, no vendor bias |
| Incumbent_score ≤ 3 flag (straw-man) | ✅ | Lowest incumbent = 4 |
| Vendor_score ≥ 9 flag (marketing-inflation) | ✅ | Max vendor = 7 |
| Kano market-appropriateness | ✅ | Attractive V1 has vendor>incumbent; V3 attractive but inferior — flagged in rationale |
| Rationale references specific specs (MW, MWh, standards) | ✅ | VDE-AR-N 4110, IEC 80005-1, 350 kW dispensers, 20 MWh ECT reference |
| Advantage arithmetic (gap>1 = superior/inferior) | ✅ | V1 gap=3, V2 gap=0, V3 gap=1 → classifications correct |

---

sections.title: Cross-step synthesis
sections.content:
| Finding | Evidence |
|---|---|
| Grid-Code Cert (F5) is the technology-level selling feature at **7.11 overall** — highest of the six | Answers N7, N9, N11, N12, N25 simultaneously |
| EMS (F4) is the only one_dimensional feature; everything else is must_be | Sophistication above commodity-FP baseline drives N2, N15, N18 |
| 9 product-related needs from salt-air + Li-ion + MV + cert-lead — Mein Solar's pre-investment is ~€100k IP-hardening + Battery-Passport tooling | Per JTBD takeaway §5 |
| PV+BESS hybrid (V1) is the only clear vendor advantage — **superior +3 pts** over best incumbent | Leverage PV-EPC incumbency in bid |
| Single-source EPC (V3) is **inferior** to Siemens/ABB at public-port tender scale — first-reference joint-bid strategy required | Matches N10 gap |

---

rows.Value: Deliver operational grid-connected electrical energy storage assets as turnkey capital projects with single-source EPC + grid-code certification
Containerised Li-ion + PCS + MV + EMS under single EPC contract, VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8
N1 sizing 10/3/17 · N4 DSO blocker 10/3/17 · N7 RtB<15mo 10/3/17 · N10 VOB/VgV bid 9/2/16 · N25 AFIR miss 9/3/15 · N9 VdS fire 9/3/15 · N16 PCS ride-through 10/5/15

rows.Rationale (Kano · drivers · market evide: Container BESS is the commodity baseline (must_be). Primary drivers: Reliability (8, drives AFIR OPS uptime N14/N16), Time (7, modular deploy beats grid reinforcement A2). Safety muted (4) — salt-air + passenger-berth fire clearance add risk (N6/N9).
4-quadrant PCS is table-stakes. Drivers: Reliability (9, ride-through under grid fault — N16), Cost (8, arbitrage + demand-shaving — N15). Skill/Pain low — operators don't touch PCS daily.

MV coupling is expected in any port EPC. Drivers: Reliability (9), Safety (7, protection relays prevent fault propagation into terminal). Time/Cost middling because MV cabinet is not the bottleneck — DSO capacity is (N4).

EMS fit varies by sophistication — one_dimensional. Drivers: Cost (9, multi-revenue stacking N2/N15), Stress (7, reduces manual dispatch anxiety), Delight (5, the "this just works" lever for N18 dispatch-priority drift).

Highest-scoring feature — Cert delivery directly answers the #1 binding blocker (N7, N9, N11, N12). Drivers: Time (9, critical-path compression), Confidence (9, tender-evaluation gate), Stress (9, N25 AFIR-miss anxiety). Must_be per commodity FP.
Generic civil scope; any EPC provides it. Drivers: Reliability (5 — foundation + fencing are hygiene). Low Delight (2). Must_be but weakest overall differentiator.

rows.Note: Commodity-fulfilling features = must_be; EMS optimization excess = one_dim

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of IP55/66 enclosure breach from salt-air chloride corrosion (IEC 60721-3 C5-M/CX) accelerating BMS/PCS electronics failure
rows.Source constraint: Ambient Temperature Envelope + Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of Feuerwehr/VdS fire-clearance refusal for Li-ion containers sited within cruise/passenger-berth exclusion zones
Minimize the time to redesign container layout when ISPS-restricted-zone or port-police clearances reduce the available quayside footprint below 40 m²/MWh
Minimize the likelihood of accelerated cycle-life degradation from sustained high C-rate (ferry fast-charge, STS regen) exceeding LFP 0.5 C design point
Minimize the likelihood of MV protection-relay mis-coordination between DSO feeder, terminal ring-bus and OPS ship-side breakers causing nuisance trips during fault ride-through
Minimize the likelihood of firmware-version drift between BMS, PCS, EMS and port SCADA/TOS that blocks re-commissioning after a single subsystem update
Minimize the time to recover contracted SoC window after a high-C-rate cruise-berth discharge event before the next vessel call
Minimize the likelihood of EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 Battery Passport / EoL reporting non-compliance under public-asset ownership
Minimize the likelihood of HVAC condensate / salt-fog ingress degrading liquid-cooling loop seals and triggering thermal derating in summer berth peaks

rows.Check: Litmus: each need disappears if tech ≠ containerised Li-ion on coastal MV grid
rows.Status: ✅ (flow-batt removes PR4/PR7; HV direct-connect removes PR5/PR6; non-Li chemistry removes PR2/PR8)

rows.Check: No duplicate with market needs (Step 06 / JTBD)
Completeness: every hard constraint generates ≥1 need
rows.Status: ✅ (MV cap → PR5; siting → PR3; fire → PR2; thermal runaway → PR2; temp → PR1/PR9; cycle life → PR4/PR7; cert lead → PR6; supply chain → PR8)

rows.Rationale: Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency allows one VDE-AR-N 4110 dossier, one MV cabinet and one EMS covering rooftop PV + quayside BESS — directly attacks N2 (bankable multi-revenue model) and N14 (OPS uptime when PV fills SoC between berths). Schneider/Siemens bundle PV+storage but without PV-EPC craftsmanship in the same business unit. Attractive because port authorities don't expect PV-heritage EPCs to also deliver BESS at scale. Spec gap: Mein Solar 4.5 MW / 9 MWh reference + PV EPC backlog vs. Schneider bundled reference at port scale is sparse in DACH.

Mein Solar's HPC-buffer feature (350 kW × 6 on 400 kVA feeder) matches ABB's commercial HPC-BESS integration for reach-stacker / ferry-charging (N4, N15). ABB ships shipped 20 MWh at ECT Rotterdam; Mein Solar lacks port reference. Parity — neither clearly wins until a reference project closes the gap.

Siemens SI can wrap OPS (IEC 80005-1), MV feeder (VDE-AR-N 4110), BESS and TOS uplink under one Smart Infrastructure master agreement — a capability Mein Solar does not yet possess at port-public-tender scale. N10 (VOB/VgV bid without public-port reference) and N25 (AFIR-miss anxiety) both favour Siemens unless Mein Solar joint-bids. Inferior until a first HHLA/NPorts/Duisport anchor closes.

rows.Note: Attractive V1 has vendor>incumbent; V3 attractive but inferior — flagged in rationale

rows.Check: Rationale references specific specs (MW, MWh, standards)
rows.Note: VDE-AR-N 4110, IEC 80005-1, 350 kW dispensers, 20 MWh ECT reference
V1 gap=3, V2 gap=0, V3 gap=1 → classifications correct

rows.Finding: Grid-Code Cert (F5) is the technology-level selling feature at **7.11 overall** — highest of the six
rows.Evidence: Answers N7, N9, N11, N12, N25 simultaneously

rows.Finding: EMS (F4) is the only one_dimensional feature; everything else is must_be
rows.Evidence: Sophistication above commodity-FP baseline drives N2, N15, N18

rows.Finding: 9 product-related needs from salt-air + Li-ion + MV + cert-lead — Mein Solar's pre-investment is ~€100k IP-hardening + Battery-Passport tooling
PV+BESS hybrid (V1) is the only clear vendor advantage — **superior +3 pts** over best incumbent
Single-source EPC (V3) is **inferior** to Siemens/ABB at public-port tender scale — first-reference joint-bid strategy required

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.feature_description: Deliver co-located PV generator and BESS asset under one VDE-AR-N 4110 dossier, one MV cabinet and one EMS with PV-EPC craftsmanship retained in the BESS design team
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.market_name: Port Electrification — Shore Power, Crane Smoothing, Ferry & Terminal-Vehicle Charging
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.rationale: Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency permits a single grid-connection dossier and shared EMS across rooftop PV + quayside BESS — directly answers N2 (bankable multi-revenue model) and N14 (OPS uptime when PV refills SoC between berths). Schneider and Siemens bundle PV+storage but without PV-EPC craftsmanship in the same business unit. Spec gap: Mein Solar 4.5 MW / 9 MWh reference + live PV-EPC backlog vs. sparse Schneider bundled references at DACH port scale.

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.feature_description: Size BESS to supply 350 kW per dispenser peaks for reach-stacker / terminal-tractor / ferry HPC while drawing smoothed average from a weak 400 kVA MV feeder
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.market_name: Port Electrification — Shore Power, Crane Smoothing, Ferry & Terminal-Vehicle Charging
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.rationale: Mein Solar's HPC-buffer design (350 kW × 6 on 400 kVA feeder) matches ABB's commercial HPC+BESS integration for reach-stacker and ferry charging (attacks N4 DSO blocker, N15 demand-charge ceiling). ABB shipped 20 MWh at ECT Rotterdam; Mein Solar lacks a port reference of equivalent scale. Parity — neither side clearly wins until a reference project closes the gap.

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.feature_description: Wrap BESS + PCS + MV + EMS + civil + grid-code certification under one legal counterparty with a single performance guarantee (kWh throughput, availability, OPS uptime)
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.market_name: Port Electrification — Shore Power, Crane Smoothing, Ferry & Terminal-Vehicle Charging
step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.rationale: Siemens SI wraps OPS (IEC 80005-1), MV feeder (VDE-AR-N 4110), BESS and TOS uplink under one Smart Infrastructure master agreement at public-tender scale — a capability Mein Solar does not yet hold at port-public-tender scale. N10 (VOB/VgV without public-port reference) and N25 (AFIR-2030-miss anxiety) both favour Siemens until a first HHLA/NPorts/Duisport anchor closes. Attractive Kano because port authorities value single-throat-to-choke — the feature is desired but Mein Solar's implementation is inferior.

strategic_implications: Lead the bid with Grid-Code Cert Delivery (F5) and EMS sophistication (F4) — they are the technology-level differentiators that attack the top-5 underserved needs (N7, N9, N11, N12, N25)
Pre-invest ~€100k in IP55→66 salt-air hardening + Battery Passport tooling to neutralize PR1, PR8, PR9 before tender qualification
V1 PV+Storage Design Authority is the only superior vendor advantage — use Mein Solar PV-EPC portfolio as the tender-qualification anchor
V3 Single-Source EPC is inferior at public-port scale — joint-bid with Cavotec (OPS) or T1 (Siemens/ABB) for the first HHLA/NPorts/Duisport reference, then transition to stand-alone bids
